title: Coordinate-generated map field: replace 1,024 free-parameter cell templates with a shared position-conditioned embedding generator
hypothesis: Three consecutive reverts (36: hardest-impostor margin hinge on the 1024-way field; 37: K=8 multi-hypothesis regression replacing the field; 38: rejected outright for touching only the trunk's input channels while leaving the field, decode, gate, and confidence self-reported unchanged) all left one thing untouched across the entire 38-round history: the map-cell scorer itself is a flat nn.Linear(560, 1024) -- exactly 1,024 completely independent weight rows, unchanged in kind since exp 2/10, each fit from scratch with zero structural relationship to its neighbors. Exp 26 named the resulting signature precisely: since exp 20/25, train-split and eval-split unfiltered medians are EQUAL (~1 km in every area/bucket) -- the model cannot even fit its OWN training locations, which is the textbook signature of a statistically weak output parameterization, not of missing capacity (exps 26/28 tripled trunk depth three times, all reverted, two after coverage was honestly measured), missing optimization (exp 25 trained to a genuine loss plateau and moved the metric only 5%), or a weak objective (exps 34/36's hardest-impostor margin hinge, twice refuted). The one experiment that DID attack the parameterization directly -- exp 31's neural-map correlation field -- was reverted, but its own pre-registered failure mode was orthogonal to weak-parameterization itself: a spatial 4x4 crop kernel cross-correlated against a map raster is sensitive to the residual +/-45 degree heading error left after the C4 vote, which smears correlation peaks regardless of how good the underlying map representation is. That failure mode does not apply to a HOLISTIC embedding comparison (a single 64-d crop vector dotted against a single 64-d cell vector, exactly the same shape of comparison the current flat head already makes) -- so the parameterization hypothesis is genuinely untested in a form that avoids exp 31's specific breakage. This experiment replaces the flat table with a coordinate-conditioned generator: a fixed Fourier positional encoding of each cell's (u, v) center feeds a tiny shared 2-layer MLP that outputs all 1,024 cell embeddings, so a gradient at one map location updates the SAME ~6k shared parameters that determine every other cell's embedding -- an explicit smoothness/parameter-sharing prior across map positions that a bank of 1,024 independent rows structurally cannot have, directly targeting the diagnosed 'cannot fit own training locations' signature. Per the pivot directive this also forces (not as an afterthought, but as a structural consequence of deleting the flat head) a rewrite of every downstream stage that read it: the two-expert brightness gate (rd 12) is replaced by continuous FiLM conditioning of the embedding MLP; the feature extractor now taps the trunk's stride-8 block (16x16x24) as a second GAP summary alongside the stride-16 block, since a metric-learning embedding benefits from a multi-scale descriptor in a way a single flat classifier never got to exploit; decode and confidence are recomputed over the new cosine-similarity field, with confidence now reading a peak-vs-second-peak embedding margin instead of the old hand-crafted shape detector.
method: model/model.py: delete loc_logits, dark_logits, and gate (the flat linear head, the dark expert, and the brightness blend -- roughly 625k of the model's 823k parameters). Split the pretrained trunk into features_early = full[:4] (stride-8, 24ch @ 16x16) and features_late = full[4:] (stride-16, 48ch @ 8x8) -- same loaded weights, just referenced as two slices. Add: an embed_hidden/embed_out MLP (584-d descriptor [late GAP 48 + skip GAP 24 + layout 512] -> 128 -> 64, L2-normalized) producing the crop embedding; a film_mlp ([brightness, late GAP] -> gamma/beta, 128-d each) FiLM-modulating the embedding MLP's hidden layer; a fixed Fourier positional encoding of the 1,024 grid-cell centers (registered buffer, L=6 frequencies) feeding a small map_gen MLP (26 -> 64 -> 64, L2-normalized) that generates all 1,024 cell embeddings fresh every forward call; a learned scalar logit_scale (init 10.0) temperature-scaling the cosine-similarity matmul (crop_embedding @ map_embeddings.T) into the same-shaped [N, 1024] logits the rest of the pipeline (C4 vote, Gaussian-CE, sharpened soft-argmax decode) already consumes unchanged. Confidence head's input swaps the old decode-gap feature for a peak-vs-second-peak cosine-similarity margin (torch.topk(2)), kept alongside peak mass and entropy. model/train.py: exactly one line changes -- `trunk_params = list(model.features.parameters())` becomes `list(model.features_early.parameters()) + list(model.features_late.parameters())` -- sampler, calibration, schedule, and loss_fn's math are byte-identical.
expected_outcome: If the flat table's independent-row parameterization is the binding constraint the last three refutations jointly point at, the worst-case median improves from 743.07 m to roughly 620-720 m (3-16%), with the clearest mechanism-level signature being train-split and eval-split unfiltered medians FINALLY diverging (train dropping measurably below eval) instead of the tied ~1 km both have shown since exp 20/25 -- direct evidence the shared-parameter generator can fit training locations the independent-row table could not. A matched-protocol rerun of the iter-12 field probe should show the true-cell top-1 rate rise measurably above its ~2% baseline. Falsification forks: (a) if the metric is flat/regresses AND train-split/eval-split medians stay tied, the weak-parameterization hypothesis is cleanly refuted -- the wall is descriptor information content (crop-embedding capacity), not how the map side is parameterized, redirecting the next iteration at richer crop features rather than any further map-side reparameterization; (b) if the primary metric improves but top-1 rate barely moves, parameter sharing bought regularization/generalization without fixing the specific confusable-lookalike ranking failure -- a real, bankable win whose natural next step is a margin term reapplied in THIS embedding space (materially different context from exps 34/36's refuted flat-table margin hinge); (c) if the metric regresses sharply, check via the same iter-12-style probes whether the failure mode is a globally over-smoothed field (parameter sharing collapsing spatial resolution) rather than exp 31's heading-misalignment smearing, to keep the two parameterization attempts' failure modes cleanly distinguished.
eli5: For 38 rounds the model has picked a location by keeping a giant multiple-choice answer sheet: 1,024 separate, totally independent answers, one per map tile, that it must learn from scratch with no help from each other. That's like memorizing 1,024 unrelated flashcards instead of learning a map. This experiment throws the flashcards away and replaces them with a tiny function that GENERATES an answer for any tile just from its map coordinates -- so learning about one tile automatically improves nearby and similar tiles too, instead of each one starting from zero. The photo still gets turned into its own compact fingerprint, and the guess is just 'which generated tile-fingerprint looks most like mine' -- same idea as before, just with a far smaller, far smarter answer sheet.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side; the 4 resulting similarity maps are averaged instead of 4 flat logit vectors

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small trunk, now read at TWO depths: the existing final stage (8×8×48, stride 16) plus a new tap right after the stride-8 block (16×16×24) whose own average is folded into the descriptor

stages.name: Lighting conditioning
stages.detail: a tiny network reads the crop's own brightness plus its texture summary and produces a scale+shift pair that continuously reshapes the crop's fingerprint for the current light level, replacing the old either/or blend of two separate scorers

stages.name: Crop embedding
stages.detail: the layout code, texture summary, and the new stride-8 summary are combined and squeezed into one compact 64-number fingerprint of the photo

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: instead of one big lookup table with 1,024 independent trained answers, a tiny generator turns each map tile's own coordinates into a 64-number fingerprint for that tile (shared math, not 1,024 free-standing answers); the photo's fingerprint is compared against every tile's generated fingerprint by simple similarity, then all four turned views' similarity maps are averaged

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the similarity map's contrast is turned up first, then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — same idea as before, now applied to the new similarity-based map

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: the model compares its best guess's similarity score against its second-best, distinct guess — a big gap between them means sure, a close call means unsure — and below a tuned bar it says 'no fix' instead of guessing

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: the usual grading (reward the true spot's similarity score, plus a position term and a confidence term) is kept in form, but now grades the new similarity-based map instead of raw lookup-table scores

implementation_brief:
PIVOT ROUND — every non-frozen stage listed above changes as a structural consequence of one core edit: deleting the flat 1,024-row classifier and replacing it with a coordinate-conditioned generator. Files: model/model.py (architecture) and model/train.py (exactly one line). Fixed contracts to preserve, restated: train.py's CLI stays `python -m model.train --area <a> --out-dir <d> [--data-dir <d>] [--epochs N] [--max-crops-per-bucket N] [--seed N]`; the ONNX export contract in model/model.py's docstring stays: one ONNX per area at <out>/models/<area>.onnx taking a 1x3x128x128 float32 input in [0,1] and returning [[u, v, conf]] with u, v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1], passing pipeline/score.py's frozen gates (ONNX <= 4 MiB, host latency proxy <= 250 ms). Do not touch pipeline/*.py or any /FROZEN file.

=== model/model.py ===

1) New module constants (near GRID_K etc.): EMBED_DIM = 64; FOURIER_L = 6; GEN_HIDDEN = 64; FILM_HIDDEN = 32; LOGIT_SCALE_INIT = 10.0.

2) New helper function, placed near _grid_centers:
   def _fourier_features(uv, L):
       # uv: [...,2] tensor in [0,1]
       feats = [uv]
       for l in range(L):
           freq = (2.0 ** l) * np.pi
           feats.append(torch.sin(freq * uv))
           feats.append(torch.cos(freq * uv))
       return torch.cat(feats, dim=-1)   # [..., 2*(2L+1)] = 26-d for L=6

3) Keep _build_pretrained_trunk() exactly as-is (still returns the full features[:9], pretrained weights loaded strict) — do not change its signature or loading logic.

4) In TinyLocNet.__init__, replace the single `self.features = _build_pretrained_trunk()` line and everything below it in __init__ with:
   full_trunk = _build_pretrained_trunk()
   self.features_early = full_trunk[:4]   # stride-8, 24ch @ 16x16 -- SAME submodule objects,
   self.features_late = full_trunk[4:]    # stride-16, 48ch @ 8x8 -- pretrained weights shared,
                                            # not copied, by slicing an nn.Sequential
   self.register_buffer('norm_mean', ...); self.register_buffer('norm_std', ...)  # unchanged
   with torch.no_grad():
       probe_skip = self.features_early(torch.zeros(1, 3, 128, 128))
       probe_late = self.features_late(probe_skip)
   skip_ch, skip_hw = probe_skip.shape[1], probe_skip.shape[2]     # 24, 16
   feat_ch, fmap_hw = probe_late.shape[1], probe_late.shape[2]     # 48, 8
   self.layout_squeeze = nn.Conv2d(feat_ch, layout_ch, 1)          # unchanged (layout_ch=8)
   desc_dim = feat_ch + skip_ch + layout_ch * fmap_hw * fmap_hw    # 48+24+512=584
   self.embed_hidden = nn.Linear(desc_dim, 128)
   self.embed_out = nn.Linear(128, EMBED_DIM)
   self.film_mlp = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(1 + feat_ch, FILM_HIDDEN), nn.ReLU(),
                                 nn.Linear(FILM_HIDDEN, 2 * 128))
   self.map_gen = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(2 * (2 * FOURIER_L + 1), GEN_HIDDEN), nn.ReLU(),
                                nn.Linear(GEN_HIDDEN, EMBED_DIM))
   self.logit_scale = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(float(LOGIT_SCALE_INIT)))
   self.conf_head = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(feat_ch + 4, 32), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(32, 1))
   self.register_buffer('conf_shift', torch.zeros(1))
   cell_u, cell_v = _grid_centers(grid_k)
   self.register_buffer('cell_u', cell_u); self.register_buffer('cell_v', cell_v)
   self.register_buffer('cell_fourier', _fourier_features(torch.stack([cell_u, cell_v], dim=1), FOURIER_L))
   DELETE: self.loc_logits, self.dark_logits, self.gate (and layout_squeeze's old feat_ch probe based on self.features — replaced above).

5) Replace forward() body with:
   n = x.shape[0]
   lum = x.mean(dim=(1, 2, 3)).unsqueeze(1)
   x4 = _c4_stack(x)
   xin = (x4 - self.norm_mean) / self.norm_std
   skip = self.features_early(xin)
   late = self.features_late(skip)
   late_gap = late.mean(dim=(2, 3))
   skip_gap = skip.mean(dim=(2, 3))
   layout = self.layout_squeeze(late).flatten(1)
   desc = torch.cat([late_gap, skip_gap, layout], dim=1)
   lum4 = lum.repeat(4, 1)
   gb = self.film_mlp(torch.cat([lum4, late_gap], dim=1))
   gamma, beta = gb[:, :128], gb[:, 128:]
   h = torch.relu(self.embed_hidden(desc))
   h = gamma * h + beta
   emb = nn.functional.normalize(self.embed_out(h), dim=1)
   map_emb = nn.functional.normalize(self.map_gen(self.cell_fourier), dim=1)   # [1024, 64]
   logits4 = self.logit_scale * (emb @ map_emb.t())                             # [4N, 1024]
   logits = logits4.reshape(4, n, -1).mean(dim=0)
   late_gap_avg = late_gap.reshape(4, n, -1).mean(dim=0)
   p = torch.softmax(DECODE_BETA * logits, dim=1)
   u = (p * self.cell_u).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True); v = (p * self.cell_v).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True)
   p1 = torch.softmax(logits, dim=1)
   u1 = (p1 * self.cell_u).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True); v1 = (p1 * self.cell_v).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True)
   pd = p.detach()
   peak = pd.max(dim=1, keepdim=True).values
   ent = -(pd * torch.log(pd + 1e-9)).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True) / float(np.log(self.grid_k ** 2))
   gapdec = torch.sqrt((u.detach() - u1.detach()) ** 2 + (v.detach() - v1.detach()) ** 2 + 1e-12)
   raw_sim = (logits / self.logit_scale).detach()
   top2 = raw_sim.topk(2, dim=1).values
   margin = top2[:, 0:1] - top2[:, 1:2]
   z = self.conf_head(torch.cat([late_gap_avg.detach(), peak, ent, gapdec, margin], dim=1))
   conf = torch.sigmoid(z - self.conf_shift)
   out = torch.cat([u, v, conf], dim=1)
   return (out, logits) if return_logits else out
   (conf_head input is feat_ch+4=52-d: late_gap_avg(48)+peak(1)+ent(1)+gapdec(1)+margin(1), matching the nn.Linear(feat_ch + 4, 32) declared in __init__ above.)

6) loss_fn: NO CHANGES to its logic — it already takes `logits` as an opaque [N,1024] tensor and applies Gaussian-smoothed CE + coord L2 + conf BCE; only note in its docstring that `logits` are now temperature-scaled cosine similarities rather than raw linear outputs. export_onnx: unchanged. F.normalize, torch.topk, torch.sin/cos all export cleanly under opset 17 with dynamo=False; if topk gives ONNX export trouble, fall back to two sequential `.max(dim=1)` passes (mask out the top-1 index, take max again) for the margin term.

=== model/train.py ===

7) The ONLY change: `trunk_params = list(model.features.parameters())` becomes `trunk_params = list(model.features_early.parameters()) + list(model.features_late.parameters())` (model.features no longer exists as an attribute). Everything else — sample_epoch, compute_cell_weights, prepare_realizations, calibrate_conf_shift, the epoch loop, the cosine schedule, EPOCH_MULT, CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA — is byte-identical. Optionally update the `info` dict's "init" string to mention the stride-8 tap for lineage clarity (not required).

Parameter budget sanity check (do not need to re-verify, just context): removing loc_logits (~574k params) and dark_logits+gate (~50k) while adding embed_hidden/out (~83k), film_mlp (~10k), map_gen (~6k), conf_head (~1.7k) nets a LARGE parameter reduction (~823k -> ~291k total including the unchanged ~190k pretrained trunk), so the 4 MiB ONNX gate has generous margin at fp32 — no int8 quantization needed this round.
